EvidĂȘncia: `node --test packages/pi-stack/test/monitor-provider-patch.test.mjs` (25/25 pass), `npm run verify` (10 checks pass), e smoke controlado com `monitors-control on` + 3 ciclos tool_call (unauthorized-action) sem novo incremento de `classifyFail` em `monitors_compact_status`. [rationale:risk-control] Turns git gc warning interpretation into deterministic policy without running git prune/gc automatically.

Added `Escada mĂ­nima para sinais simples` to docs/guides/control-plane-operating-doctrine.md. The doctrine now says simple signals should be observed/classified, fixed in the current slice only when local-safe, small, reversible by git, and bounded-validatable, recorded when relevant, escalated for explicit authorization when destructive/protected/external/costly/irreversible, turned into a task/decision when structural, and hardened when recurring. It explicitly states 'simples' does not mean automatic. For git maintenance, diagnosing/recording/recommending is allowed, while `git gc`, `git prune`, and removing `.git/gc.log` require explicit authorization. Validation: corrected safe_marker_check passed 10/10 after one over-specific marker mismatch; focal git-maintenance/context-watchdog smoke passed 54/54. [rationale:risk-control] This creates the basic decision ladder requested by the operator without authorizing destructive maintenance or adding operational fat.

Defined long-term .project strategy in docs/guides/project-canonical-pipeline.md and linked it from docs/guides/control-plane-operating-doctrine.md. The doctrine now states .project is the current local-first adapter, not mandatory final storage; canonical semantics come first and storage remains replaceable. It maps roles for tasks, verification, handoff, auxiliary decision blocks, and adapter/cache behavior. It distinguishes hard intent local usage (local task close, verification gates, handoff continuity, one local slice with clear ownership through bounded surfaces) from soft evidence usage (external selected systems, mirrors, stale focus, multiple writers, remote/CI/colony without ownership). It defines mode/ownership boundaries for single local agent, multiple local agents, worktree/colony, remote/offload, GitHub Actions/CI, external issue trackers, and Markdown/Obsidian; conflict rules fail closed to checkpoint/ask/promotion gates rather than overwrite. It states before executor/repetition/scheduler depend on .project as authority, ownership/stale-evidence mode must be explicit. Validation: safe_marker_check passed 14/14 for project-canonical-pipeline and 5/5 for operating doctrine after initial over-specific marker mismatch; focal project-board/context-watchdog smoke passed 102/102. [rationale:architecture] This answers the operator concern by preventing .project from becoming lock-in, noise, or ambiguous authority across agent modes.

verifications.method: github-actions protected canary (single run) + board evidence
verifications.evidence: Human decision: `promote` received and executed as one protected canary. Canary envelope (pre-run): - owner: aretw0 - scope: github-actions/publish-workflow - ref: main - maxCostUsd: 2 - expectedValue: validate protected/offload path with auditable run id + terminal conclusion. - focalValidationGate: `gh run view <id>` terminal result. - rollbackPlan: stop at one run; keep no-auto-dispatch; if degraded, record `defer` and return to local-safe lane. Execution (exactly 1 run): - command: `gh workflow run Publish --ref main` - run url: https://github.com/aretw0/agents-lab/actions/runs/25254616551 - run id: 25254616551 - event: workflow_dispatch - status/conclusion: completed/success - timestamps: createdAt=2026-05-02T14:53:29Z updatedAt=2026-05-02T14:53:46Z (~0.28 min) - job id: 74051885583 (`Publish to npm`) - guard signal: log contains `No semver tag found ...; skipping publish.` - publish steps remained skipped (`Verificar tag...`, `Instalar dependĂȘncias`, `Revalidar gates...`, `Publicar pacotes`). Decision evidence (post-canary): - decision promote executed for single canary. - decision for expansion: defer (no automatic expansion after first protected canary; keep bounded/manual). - cost evidence: budget envelope maxCostUsd=2 respected; observed direct publish cost path not entered (steps skipped), effective run cost treated as near-zero for this canary. Result: - Acceptance criteria met: single bounded protected run, explicit budget/gate, board-level job/result/cost/decision evidence, rollback readiness preserved without auto-dispatch.

Implemented report-first change discovery in CI (inspired by refarm organization) without reducing coverage. Changes: - `.github/workflows/ci.yml` - Added new job `changes` (report-only) with outputs: - `base_sha`, `head_sha`, `changed_count`, `ci_changes`, `code_changes`, `docs_only`. - Detection logic: - resolves base/head for PR and push, - handles missing/zero base SHA with full-gate fallback, - classifies changed files into CI/code/docs-only buckets, - writes summary to `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`. - `smoke` job now depends on `changes` and prints discovery summary before running gate. - Policy preserved: full canonical gate still runs (`npm run ci:smoke:gate`) even for docs-only changes. Outcome: - Better observability and organization for localâActions drift triage. - No auto-skip behavior introduced in this phase (report-first).
